testcase.name: cli > placeholder test - Phase 1 only verifies bootstrap works
runInit > package manager detection > detects pnpm from pnpm-lock.yaml
runInit > package manager detection > detects yarn from yarn.lock
runInit > package manager detection > detects npm from package-lock.json
runInit > package manager detection > defaults to npm when no lockfile exists
runInit > dry-run mode > does not create files in dry-run mode
runInit > normal mode > creates files in empty directory
runInit > normal mode > skips existing files without --force
runInit > force mode > overwrites existing files with --force
runInit > path traversal protection > blocks path traversal with ..
runInit > path traversal protection > blocks traversal to sibling directory
runInit > error handling > returns error for invalid base path
runInit > file content > creates valid contract.json
runInit > file content > includes package manager in workflow
EXIT_CODES > defines expected exit codes
--track flag > creates manifest for new files
--track flag > creates backups for existing files
--track flag > rejects symlinks with error
--rollback flag > restores created files (deletes them)
--rollback flag > restores modified files from backup
--rollback flag > fails when no manifest exists
--rollback flag > fails when manifest is corrupted
--rollback flag > blocks path traversal in manifest
--check-updates flag > fails when no manifest exists
--check-updates flag > reports update available for old version
--check-updates flag > reports up to date for same version
--check-updates flag > defaults to 0.0.0 for manifest without version
--update flag > fails when no manifest exists
--update flag > updates files and manifest version
--update flag > is no-op when already up to date
--interactive flag > returns proposed changes without writing files
--interactive flag > marks new files as 'create' action
--interactive flag > marks existing files as 'skip' action without --force
--interactive flag > marks existing files as 'modify' action with --force
--interactive flag > detects package manager for rendering
generateDiff > generates diff for new file (create action)
generateDiff > generates diff for modified file
generateDiff > returns empty string for skip action
--migrate flag > fails when no contract exists
--migrate flag > succeeds when contract is already at latest version
--migrate flag > fails when contract has invalid JSON
--migrate flag > fails when contract is missing version field
--migrate flag > preserves user customizations during migration
--migrate flag > preserves contract content when already up to date
detectContractVersion > returns null when contract doesn't exist
detectContractVersion > returns version from valid contract
detectContractVersion > returns null for contract without version
detectContractVersion > returns null for invalid JSON
runPolicyGate > with max-tier > passes when tier equals max-tier
runPolicyGate > with max-tier > passes when tier is lower than max-tier
runPolicyGate > with max-tier > fails when tier exceeds max-tier
runPolicyGate > with max-tier > passes high-tier files when max-tier is high
runPolicyGate > without max-tier > passes all files when no max-tier specified
runPolicyGate > validation > rejects invalid max-tier value
runPolicyGate > validation > returns error for missing contract
runPolicyGateCLI > returns SUCCESS (0) when gate passes
runPolicyGateCLI > returns POLICY_VIOLATION (1) when gate fails
runPolicyGateCLI > returns VALIDATION_ERROR (1) for invalid max-tier
testcase.system-err: Invalid max-tier: must be one of high, medium, low {"error":{"code":"VALIDATION_ERROR","message":"Invalid max-tier: must be one of high, medium, low"}}

testcase.name: runReviewGate > returns validation error for invalid SHA
runReviewGate > returns not_found when check run does not exist
runReviewGate > returns verified when check run is completed with success
runReviewGate > returns needsRerun when check run is completed with failure
runReviewGate > returns error on timeout with fail action
validateContract > accepts minimal valid contract with version only
validateContract > applies defaults for optional fields
validateContract > rejects invalid risk tier
validateContract > rejects missing version
validateContract > accumulates multiple errors
validateContract > accepts valid risk tier rules
validateContract > rejects __proto__ key (prototype pollution)
validateContract > rejects constructor key (prototype pollution)
sanitizeError > redacts GitHub PATs
sanitizeError > redacts home paths
sanitizeError > preserves commit hashes (not 20+ alphanumeric)
escapeMarkdown > escapes backslashes
escapeMarkdown > escapes backticks
escapeMarkdown > escapes asterisks and underscores
escapeMarkdown > escapes angle brackets
escapeMarkdown > escapes @ mentions with zero-width space
escapeMarkdown > escapes URLs with zero-width space
escapeMarkdown > escapes multiple special characters together
formatRerunComment > creates valid comment for valid SHA
formatRerunComment > escapes markdown in reason
formatRerunComment > throws for invalid SHA
formatRerunComment > includes timestamp in ISO format
hasRerunCommentForSha > returns found:false when no comments match
hasRerunCommentForSha > returns found:false for comments from other users
hasRerunCommentForSha > returns found:false for comments without marker
hasRerunCommentForSha > returns found:false for comments older than 24 hours
hasRerunCommentForSha > returns found:true for matching comment within 24 hours
hasRerunCommentForSha > returns found:false for different SHA
hasRerunCommentForSha > returns found:false for invalid SHA input
validateSha > accepts valid 40-character lowercase hex SHA
validateSha > rejects SHA with uppercase letters
validateSha > rejects SHA that is too short
validateSha > rejects SHA that is too long
validateSha > rejects SHA with invalid characters
validateSha > rejects empty string
validateSha > throws ShaValidationError with correct message
isValidSha > returns true for valid SHA
isValidSha > returns false for invalid SHA
isValidSha > returns false for non-string values
isValidSha > acts as type guard
createResolver > classifies auth files as high-risk
createResolver > classifies test files as low-risk
createResolver > defaults to medium for unknown paths
createResolver > handles nested paths with **
resolveOverallTier > returns high when any file is high-risk
resolveOverallTier > returns medium when all files are medium or lower
resolveOverallTier > returns medium for empty file list
